vocab.meaning: mua một món đồ mà không xem kỹ (mua hớ, mua lầm)
vocab.example: Buying shoes from an unverified social media page is like buying a pig in a poke; you never know what will actually arrive.

vocab.meaning: vẹn cả đôi đường (hưởng lợi từ hai tình huống khác nhau)
vocab.example: Shopping online for sneakers allows me to get the best of both worlds: competitive prices and the comfort of my own home.

vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.example: I saw a pair of designer heels online that I absolutely loved, but they would have cost me an arm and a leg.

vocab.meaning: vớ bở, gặp may lớn (khi tìm được món đồ tốt giá rẻ)
vocab.example: I really hit the jackpot last week when I found a pair of limited-edition running shoes at a seventy percent discount.

vocab.meaning: trả một cái giá quá đắt
vocab.example: If you don't compare prices across different websites, you might end up paying through the nose for basic trainers.

vocab.meaning: trông rất tuyệt vời, sang trọng
vocab.example: Even though I bought them on sale, these formal shoes make me look like a million dollars at weddings.

vocab.meaning: trở thành tâm điểm chú ý
vocab.example: The neon-colored boots I ordered online definitely stole the show when I wore them to the party.

vocab.meaning: trong tương lai (về độ bền của giày)
vocab.example: I'm hoping that these expensive loafers will still be in good condition a few years down the line.

vocab.meaning: chùn bước, lo sợ (thường dùng chơi chữ với việc mua giày)
vocab.example: I was about to click 'buy' on those expensive cleats, but I got cold feet and decided to wait for a clearance sale.
I was worried about the sizing, but when the package arrived, the leather shoes fit like a glove.
